name: John Gilmore
abstract: John Gilmore (September 28, 1931 – August 20, 1995) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, and percussionist. He was known for his tenure with the avant-garde keyboardist/bandleader Sun Ra from the 1950s to the 1990s, and led The Sun Ra Arkestra from Sun Ra's death in 1993 until his own death in 1995.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Sun Ra and His Space Arkestra

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: I walked in and there was Sun Ra, there was Marshall Allen, Pat Patrick, John Gilmore, Ronnie Boykins... Grab your horn and come to play with us tomorrow... I’m not sure when James Spaulding was in the band but he’s still with us. So ain’t but a handful of people, I would say maybe four or five at t
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: And every now and then I’d do a Monday night at the Bee Hive with people like John Gilmore. ... I booked him mostly because of John Gilmore and Pat Patrick on the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: At the time the band was John Gilmore, bass player Ronnie Boykins, Marshall Allen... I really enjoyed playing with Sun Ra
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: He brought all his members from Chicago, mainly ... John Gilmore, tenor ... And I got there in 1960.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 17, 2022 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Wendell Harrison
about_quotes.evidence: But meanwhile I would take little gigs with Sun Ra, with John Gilmore playing saxophone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendell Harrison interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 10, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: I met Steve earlier in that playing in Pete LaRoca’s quartet with John Gilmore.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
